_comment: Adversarial-review panel composition, as data. The driver (run-review.ps1 / run_review.py) reads this to know which reviewers to run, with which wrapper, model, and reasoning effort. Swap or add reviewers HERE, not in code. INVARIANT: the active (enabled) reviewer set must span at least `minVendors` distinct vendors — uncorrelated error across vendors is the entire point of the panel. A panel of same-vendor reviewers is just self-review.

wrappers._comment: Each wrapper is a sibling .ps1 with a uniform contract: -Instruction -DiffPath [-FindingsPath] [-ContextPath...] -Model, returning the review text on stdout and a non-zero exit on failure. `effort` and `repoAccess` are honoured only by wrappers that declare the matching parameter (-Effort / -RepoPath); the driver introspects each wrapper and silently omits a flag the wrapper does not support. Today only claude-review.ps1 exposes both — Copilot and Gemini CLIs do not surface a clean reasoning-effort flag, so `effort` is a no-op for them.

reviewers._comment: Anthropic reviewer slot #1 (Sonnet). Set to `sonnet` (not `opus`) deliberately: the Phase-3 judge is Opus (see `roles.judge`), so a Sonnet reviewer keeps reviewer != judge model, preserving the panel's decorrelation. An Opus reviewer would collapse the anthropic axis to the judge's model seen twice. The Opus judge + Phase-4 verifiers carry the heavy reasoning. TWO-ANTHROPIC-REVIEWER CAVEAT: since 2026-07-02 there is a second Anthropic reviewer (Fable, id `F`, below) enabled while Fable 5 is available for a limited time. Fable + Sonnet share a vendor, so their errors correlate — they are NOT two independent votes. The Phase-3 brief therefore weights consensus by VENDOR, not reviewer headcount (Fable+Sonnet agreeing = one Anthropic vote), and the Observatory telemetry sums both into one `anthropic/reviewer` row (the dashboard tracks the vendor axis). When Fable's limited-time window closes, disable id `F` and this reverts to a single Anthropic reviewer.
reviewers.label: Claude Sonnet

reviewers._comment: Anthropic reviewer slot #2 (Fable 5). Added 2026-07-02 while Fable 5 is available for a limited time. Same vendor as reviewer B — see the two-anthropic caveat on B: this is a correlated, not independent, second vote, handled by vendor-weighted consensus (Phase-3 brief) and a merged Observatory row. `model: fable` is the CLI alias (claude-review.ps1 --model / the Agent tool both accept it); the canonical id for telemetry is `claude-fable-5`. Disable this (enabled:false) when the Fable window closes to return to a single Anthropic reviewer.
reviewers.label: Claude Fable 5

alternates._comment: Opus is reserved as judge (Phase 3) and verifier — not a blind reviewer. Do NOT pair a Gemini reviewer with a Gemini-backed host adjudicating the same finding — same-vendor reviewer-and-judge re-correlates the error the panel exists to decorrelate.
alternates.label: Claude Opus

roles._comment: Judgment roles the host runs after the driver's blind+cross-examine spine. Under Claude Code these may instead run as in-process Agent-tool calls; the driver and SKILL.md describe both paths. effort/repoAccess apply as above.
